chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: ES6, also known as ECMAScript 2015, introduced many new features to JavaScript.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: We have already learned about let, const, arrow functions, and template literals.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Destructuring assignment lets you extract values from arrays and objects into variables.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Array destructuring uses square brackets. const [a, b] = [1, 2].

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Object destructuring uses curly braces. const {name, age} = person.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: The spread operator, three dots, expands an iterable into individual elements.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: The rest operator, also three dots, collects remaining elements into an array.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Default parameters allow function parameters to have default values.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: The for-of loop iterates over iterable objects like arrays and strings.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Maps are collections of key-value pairs where keys can be any data type.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Sets are collections of unique values. Duplicate values are automatically removed.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Symbols are unique and immutable data types used as object property keys.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Optional chaining allows you to safely access deeply nested properties.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Nullish coalescing operator returns the right operand when the left is null or undefined.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Object shorthand properties allow you to omit the value if it matches the variable name.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Computed property names let you use expressions as object property names.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Modules allow you to split your code into separate files using import and export.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Named exports can export multiple values. Default export exports a single value.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: The import statement is used to bring in values from other modules.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Using modules makes your code more organized, maintainable, and reusable.

chunks.title: JavaScript ES6+ Modern Features
chunks.text: Modern JavaScript is very powerful and expressive. These features will make you a better developer.

text: ES6, also known as ECMAScript 2015, introduced many new features to JavaScript. We have already learned about let, const, arrow functions, and template literals. Destructuring assignment lets you extract values from arrays and objects into variables. Array destructuring uses square brackets. const [a, b] = [1, 2]. Object destructuring uses curly braces. const {name, age} = person. The spread operator, three dots, expands an iterable into individual elements. The rest operator, also three dots, collects remaining elements into an array. Default parameters allow function parameters to have default values. The for-of loop iterates over iterable objects like arrays and strings. Maps are collections of key-value pairs where keys can be any data type. Sets are collections of unique values. Duplicate values are automatically removed. Symbols are unique and immutable data types used as object property keys. Optional chaining allows you to safely access deeply nested properties. Nullish coalescing operator returns the right operand when the left is null or undefined. Object shorthand properties allow you to omit the value if it matches the variable name. Computed property names let you use expressions as object property names. Modules allow you to split your code into separate files using import and export. Named exports can export multiple values. Default export exports a single value. The import statement is used to bring in values from other modules. Using modules makes your code more organized, maintainable, and reusable. Modern JavaScript is very powerful and expressive. These features will make you a better developer.
